title: Repository Purpose, Risk Profile, and Definition of Done · Gate Defense
summary: Design quality gates for a new repository according to its actual failure costs. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a repository engineering contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.
Rank correctness, security, accessibility, performance, compatibility, and maintenance risks.
Define machine-verifiable and human-reviewed completion criteria.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Repo Purpose · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates risk contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: gates risk contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Which action demonstrates Repo Purpose without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.

content.text: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: gates risk contract. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Repo Purpose, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a repository engineering contract.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Every new repository should use the same maximum gate set. This fails the Repo Purpose contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Clang AddressSanitizer and UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer (Clang 23 development documentation) — Controls native-code runtime sanitizer purpose, build profiles, output, limitations, and security cautions.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define users, runtime, data sensitivity, deployment, ownership, and expected lifetime.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Exception Policy applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Document bounded suppressions, owners, expiry, and review evidence.
